strengths: Proven experience building event-driven systems and real-time infrastructure across multiple domains, including high-throughput data pipelines.
Hands-on contributions to AI-agent tooling and frameworks at re:cinq, including MCP code generators supporting agentic workflows.
Strong engineering culture advocacy through workshops, TDD, CI/CD, and open-source contributions demonstrating leadership beyond code.
concerns: No explicit evidence of leading engineering teams from 10 to 30+ people or developing senior technical leadership layers.
Lack of documented experience in regulated fintech environments or building credit-risk, working-capital, or trade-decisioning systems.
Minimal mention of multi-agent architecture design, stateful reasoning over market data, or observability infrastructure for auditable AI systems.
response.justification: The candidate demonstrates strong technical depth in event-driven systems, real-time infrastructure, and emerging agentic tooling, with relevant hands-on AI-agent work—but lacks demonstrable experience in fintech-regulated environments, credit-risk systems, and scaling senior engineering leadership, which are critical for this role.
